summary: Package ships live Google Cloud service-account private key and third-party POS credentials
details: The published tarball for tango-app-api-trax@3.9.47 contains a complete Google service-account JSON file (fir-51e77-firebase-adminsdk-x3sdp-fd902b74ae.json) including an RSA private key, client_email firebase-adminsdk-k7lom@tango-trax.iam.gserviceaccount.com, and project_id tango-trax. Any installer of this package receives usable Firebase Admin credentials that grant administrative access to the tango-trax Google Cloud project (Firestore, Realtime Database, FCM, Auth, etc.). Additionally, src/controllers/internalTrax.controller.js hardcodes credentials for Lenskart POS systems (username 'tango.eye', password '55eyetango123', and X-Lenskart-API-Key 'valyoo123') used against webservice.pos.lenskart.com and central.pos.lenskart.com. The package has no install lifecycle hooks, so the credentials are not exfiltrated automatically; however, simply installing the package distributes live, usable third-party credentials to every consumer of the public npm registry, who can then impersonate the integration user against Google Cloud and Lenskart's POS infrastructure. This appears to be an internal application accidentally published to the public registry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
